title: 10 best herbal essential oils for cirrhosis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for cirrhosis are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of this liver disease by reducing inflammation, promoting liver regeneration, and improving overall liver function. The best essential oils for cirrhosis include Silybum marianum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Astragalus membranaceus, Phyllanthus niruri, Taraxacum officinale, Cynara scolymus, Schisandra chinensis, and Cinchona officinalis. If left untreated, cirrhosis can lead to liver failure, jaundice, and even death, severely impacting one's quality of life, causing fatigue, weight loss, and a range of other debilitating symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, which are key contributors to the progression of cirrhosis. Additionally, sage essential oil's ability to improve digestion and reduce oxidative stress can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, nausea, and abdominal pain commonly associated with cirrhosis. By incorporating sage essential oils into one's regimen, individuals may experience improved liver function and overall health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, common issues associated with cirrhosis.
Lipid-lowering properties: Sage has been shown to have lipid-lowering effects, which may be beneficial in managing fatty liver disease, a common complication of cirrhosis.

Antifibrotic activity: Sage extracts have been found to inhibit collagen synthesis and reduce liver fibrosis, which is the scarring process that occurs in the liver as a result of chronic liver damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of sage are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils that help reduce liver damage caused by cirrhosis.

Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit antifibrotic properties, which can help prevent the progression of cirrhosis by reducing scarring and inflammation in the liver.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves at dawn on a sunny day, ensuring freshness and potency.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by rinsing them gently with cool water to remove any impurities or debris.
Steam-distill the leaves using 10-15% distillation ratio with demineralized water for 1-2 hours, yielding approximately 0.5-1.5%.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a sterile cotton cloth or paper filter to remove any particles or impurities.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. The curcumin present in turmeric oil has been shown to improve liver function and reduce fibrosis, a common complication of cirrhosis. Additionally, turmeric's anti-inflammatory effects may help to alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, joint pain, and jaundice associated with cirrhosis. Overall, incorporating turmeric essential oils into a treatment plan may help to slow the progression of cirrhosis and improve overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcuminoids, can help reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with cirrhosis.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric essential oil's antioxidant activity can neutralize free radicals that contribute to liver damage and fibrosis (scarring) in cirrhotic patients.

Anti-fibrotic property: Turmeric essential oil's bioactive compounds, such as curcumin, have been shown to inhibit the production of collagen and prevent liver fibrosis, which can slow down disease progression.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have antifibrotic effects, helping to reduce liver scarring associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric rhizomes and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the cleaned rhizomes in a warm place for at least three days or until they become brittle.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine one part turmeric powder with four parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days shaking the jar daily then strain and store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with cirrhosis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis. The oil's active ingredients, such as glycyrrhizin, also exhibit antioxidant effects, neutralizing free radicals that damage liver cells. Additionally, licorice essential oil may stimulate the production of liver enzymes, improving liver function and detoxification processes. This natural approach has shown promise in alleviating symptoms and slowing disease progression in cirrhosis patients.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root extract's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, may help reduce inflammation in the liver, which can exacerbate cirrhotic symptoms.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of licorice root extract may help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of cirrhosis.
Hepatoprotective property: Licorice root extract has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning it may help prevent or reduce liver damage and fibrosis associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in treating liver damage caused by cirrhosis.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam distill the washed roots using a steam distillation apparatus for 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and mix it with jojoba carrier oil in a ratio of 5%.
Store the blended oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for 24 hours.
Filter the blend through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the liver inflammation associated with this condition. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant compounds have been shown to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in liver cells, protecting them from further damage. Furthermore, ginger has also been found to improve blood flow and reduce portal hypertension, a common complication of cirrhosis, which can help alleviate symptoms such as ascites and esophageal varices.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal ginger essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the liver from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the liver and other tissues, which is a key factor in cirrhosis development and progression.

Cytoprotective properties: Herbal ginger essential oil's cytoprotective properties help protect liver cells from damage and death caused by toxins, which can help prevent further liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good for making medicinal essential oils for cirrhosis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with cirrhosis.
Roots: Roots are used to make medicinal essential oils for cirrhosis due to their ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea, common symptoms associated with liver disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots with a moderate amount of fiber and rough skin.
Wash the ginger roots thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and impurities.
Slice the ginger roots into thin pieces using a sharp knife or grater to increase surface area.
Steam distill the sliced ginger roots for 60-90 minutes using a copper still or glass apparatus.
Combine the obtained essential oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:3.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and damage caused by this chronic condition. The oil's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve wound healing also aids in the repair of damaged liver tissue. Additionally, aloe vera's natural detoxifying agents can help to remove toxins and waste products from the body, further supporting the liver's function and promoting overall health and well-being.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in aloe essential oil can help to alleviate liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Wound-healing and tissue-regeneration properties: Aloe essential oil's ability to stimulate collagen production and promote tissue regeneration may help to improve liver function and structure, which is compromised in cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain bioactive compounds that help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, making them effective in treating cirrhosis-related symptoms.
Seeds: Aloe seeds are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which can aid in reducing liver damage and improving liver function in patients with cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500ml of fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves.
Blend 10g of dried turmeric powder and 5g of dried ginger powder into the gel.
Steam-distill the mixture for 4 hours using a steam distillation apparatus.
Combine 20ml of distilled water with 5 drops of lemon essential oil and mix well.
Filter the resulting oil through cheesecloth and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil has been shown to reduce liver damage by neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative stress. Additionally, lemongrass essential oil's citral content has been found to stimulate the production of glutathione, a crucial antioxidant in the body that helps remove toxins from the liver. By promoting liver health and reducing inflammation, lemongrass essential oils may help alleviate symptoms associated with cirrhosis, such as fatigue and digestive issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the liver and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms such as pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with cirrhosis.

Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that often complicate cirrhosis, reducing the risk of liver abscesses and other complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of lemongrass have been traditionally used to treat liver-related issues, making them an ideal part for creating essential oils to manage cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is a modified stem that grows underground and contains high concentrations of medicinal compounds, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from a reputable supplier or cultivate your own.
Clean and sort the harvested leaves to remove any debris or impurities carefully.
Steam distill 500g of clean cymbopogon citratus leaves in 1l of water for 4 hours patiently.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a glass separator method promptly.
Transfer the extracted essential oil into dark glass bottles and store at room temperature securely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as curcuminoids and terpenes, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, which are characteristic symptoms of cirrhosis. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum leaves from your garden or local market in the early morning.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air dry for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried helichrysum italicum leaves with 100 grams of dried herbs and 500 ml of distilled water for 4 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the distillation mixture using a coffee filter paper and store in a dark glass bottle.
Add 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the helichrysum italicum essential oil and mix well for 2 minutes.

Separate the essential oil from the distilled mixture by decanting it into a clean glass container within 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove any impurities then store in dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at peak potency during morning hours after dew has evaporated.
Steam distill fresh harvested melissa officinalis leaves in a stainless steel apparatus for 30-45 minutes.
Monitor temperature and pressure levels throughout the steam distillation process to ensure optimal conditions.
Collect and filter distilled oil through activated charcoal or diatomaceous earth to remove impurities and contaminants.
Store freshly produced melissa officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to 12 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and stems in early morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a clevenger apparatus with 10 liters of water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil using a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 6 months.
Dilute the essential oil with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 3% hypericum perforatum essential oil to 97% carrier oil.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cirrhosis with herbal essential oils is by combining certain blends of oils that promote liver health, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion. Mix equal parts of frankincense, peppermint, and ginger essential oils and apply topically or inhale through steam inhalation. This blend can help reduce liver scarring, alleviate digestive issues, and promote overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cirrhosis that are treated with herbal essential oils are liver and gallbladder disorders, such as hepatitis, jaundice, and cholecystitis. Certain essential oils like turmeric, ginger, and frankincense have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and improve overall liver function. Additionally, oils like peppermint and lemon balm can aid in digestion and reduce symptoms of gallbladder issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It contains silymarin, a complex mixture of flavonoids, which has been shown to protect the liver from damage and promote its natural regeneration. Flavonoids (especially isoorientin and isovitexin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help protect liver cells from oxidative damage and reduce liver fibrosis associated with cirrhosis.

Lignans (especially silibinin and silidianin): Lignans in Silybum marianum have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce liver inflammation and damage, and may even stimulate liver regeneration and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum seeds and flowers from mature plants in the morning to ensure peak oil quality and aroma.
Dry the harvested silybum marianum seeds and flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried silybum marianum seeds and flowers for 2-3 hours to release essential oils.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separation funnel and store in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and ensure clarity before labeling and storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to protect the liver by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which are major contributors to liver damage. Additionally, the oil's ability to modulate the immune system may help to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, promoting liver regeneration and potentially slowing the progression of cirrhosis. remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with cirrhosis by reducing liver inflammation, improving liver function, and increasing the production of liver cells.
Licoricidin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the liver from oxidative damage, reduce liver inflammation, and improve liver function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning for optimal quality.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 15%.
Steam distill the dried roots using a 1:3 root-to-water ratio for 2-3 hours to extract essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and discard the plant material to obtain 50 grams of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and obtain a clear 30-ml bottle of oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It contains curcumin, a compound that reduces liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key factors in cirrhosis development. By modulating the liver's inflammatory response and promoting tissue repair, Curcuma longa essential oils may help slow down liver fibrosis and improve liver function. This, in turn, can lead to improved overall health and a reduced risk of complications associated with cirrhosis. Regular use may also enhance liver regeneration and detoxification processes.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key factors contributing to the progression of cirrhosis.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Inhibits the growth and proliferation of liver cancer cells, which can develop from cirrhosis, and has antioxidant properties to protect the liver.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce liver damage and promote liver regeneration in cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate liver damage.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their essential oil, which is rich in beta-caryophyllene, a compound with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can aid in liver health.
Roots: Roots are another commonly used part, containing essential oils with curcuminoids and other compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes with bright yellow color and firm texture.
Dry the rhizomes in a warm place for 2-3 weeks to achieve 10-15% moisture content.
Grind the dried rhizomes into fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 2 minutes.
Steam distill the powder in a steam distillation apparatus at 100-120°c for 2-3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the condenser and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress. The oil's active compounds, gingerols, and shogaols, have been shown to improve liver function and promote regeneration of liver cells. remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-10 kg of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with thick and firm texture at peak maturity.
Steam distill the harvested rhizomes for 2-3 hours at 100-110°c to extract essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator or decanter at 20-25°c.
Filter the separated essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities at 20-25°c.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles at 20-25°c and away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus essential oils helps with cirrhosis because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and damage. remedy_constituents: Astragaloside IV: This saponin constituent has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.
Astragaloside VI: This saponin constituent has been found to have anti-apoptotic properties, which can help protect liver cells from apoptosis (programmed cell death) and promote liver regeneration.

Isorhapontigenin: This phenolic compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis, as well as inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Astragalus membranaceus are often used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of saponins and other bioactive compounds that may aid in liver health.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus roots in late summer to early fall season.
Steam distill the roots in a still with a ratio of 1:3 water to plant material at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the water through decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any impurities or contaminants from the distillation process.
Store the astragalus membranaceus essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid at room temperature.

Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the taraxacum officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus essential oils helps with cirrhosis because it contains compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce liver inflammation and damage caused by free radicals. The essential oil has been shown to improve liver function and reduce liver enzymes, which are often elevated in cirrhosis. Additionally, it may help to regenerate liver cells and improve overall liver health, making it a potential natural remedy for managing cirrhosis symptoms and promoting liver recovery.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to cirrhosis progression.
Terpenoids: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing liver damage and promoting tissue repair in cirrhosis.
Flavonoids: They possess hepatoprotective properties, improving liver function, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting regeneration of liver cells.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cynara scolymus are used to make essential oils due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing liver damage and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
Flowers: The flowers of Cynara scolymus are used to make essential oils due to their high inulin content, which can help in reducing liver fibrosis and promoting liver regeneration.
Stems: The stems of Cynara scolymus are used to make essential oils due to their flavonoid content, which can help in protecting liver cells from damage and promoting liver function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh cynara scolymus flowers in the early morning to ensure peak potency.
Steam distill the flowers at a temperature of 100°c with a condenser for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid for 2 weeks.
Analyze the essential oil for its quality and purity using gas chromatography and mass spectrometry.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis essential oils helps with cirrhosis because it has been traditionally used to promote liver health and regeneration. The oil is rich in schisandrins, lignans, and other bioactive compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to cirrhosis. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis essential oils may improve liver function by enhancing the production of enzymes involved in detoxification and reducing liver fibrosis. This may help to slow disease progression and improve overall health.
remedy_constituents: Schisandrins: These lignans have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with cirrhosis.
Schisandrin B: This lignan has been shown to improve liver function and reduce oxidative stress, helping to slow down liver fibrosis and cirrhosis progression.
Schisandrigenin: This lignan has been found to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to prevent liver cell damage and promote liver regeneration, which can help alleviate cirrhosis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits are used to make essential oils for cirrhosis because they are rich in schisandrins, compounds that may help protect the liver and improve liver function.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils for cirrhosis because they contain antioxidants and other compounds that may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress.
Barks: Barks are used to make essential oils for cirrhosis because they contain schisandrins and other compounds that may help improve liver function and protect against liver damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 kilograms of fresh schisandra chinensis berries in late summer or early autumn.
Dry the berries in a warm and well-ventilated area for 3-5 days at 40-50°c.
Steam distill the dried berries using a steam distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a coffee filter.
Store the schisandra chinensis essential oil in a dark glass bottle at a temperature below 30°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's quinine content is believed to help reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with cirrhosis. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis contains flavonoids and tannins, which have been shown to improve liver function and promote the regeneration of liver cells. This helps to alleviate symptoms and slow the progression of the disease, providing a natural and holistic approach to managing cirrhosis and promoting overall liver health.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Helps reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common factors contributing to cirrhosis progression.
Quinidine: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate liver damage and fibrosis associated with cirrhosis.
Quinic acid: Has antioxidant properties, which can neutralize free radicals that cause liver cell damage and contribute to cirrhosis development.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help in treating cirrhosis symptoms.
Barks: The barks are used due to their rich content of quinine and other compounds that aid in reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, and promoting liver health.
Stems: The stems are used for their content of cinchonine and other alkaloids that help in reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and providing relief from cirrhosis symptoms.
